campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed public hearing matters and mentioned an upcoming Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: City staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention strategies
Boogie Festival this weekend
Public hearing on government code section 3502 matter
Council reports and public comment period

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved an engineer's report and moved forward with a hearing process related to what appears to be a major infrastructure or assessment project. The council also discussed whether Saratoga should become a charter city and reviewed potential tax and revenue options.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report preliminary approval for fiscal year
Public hearing process underway
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption on May 20
Charter city status exploration and next steps
Preliminary tax options and revenue estimates being reviewed

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 19, 2026, to approve previous meeting records, review the town's finances from March, and authorize several projects and agreements. The council approved donations of safety equipment, authorized service contracts, approved a paving construction project, and identified infrastructure projects for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Approved O'Grady Paving construction contract
Accepted donation of automated external defibrillator (AED)
Reviewed March 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized new five-year services agreement
Approved list of projects for Fiscal Year 2026-2027

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 20, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agenda items. The council cancelled their May 26 meeting and instead reviewed draft items for their June 2 meeting, including planning event approvals and updating committee work plans.
keyTopics: May 26 council meeting cancelled
June 2 draft agenda review and new item additions
Neighborhood Services and Education Committee work plan update
Rules and Open Government Committee work plan review
Event sponsorship approvals

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 20, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on May 20, 2026, to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments from residents. The main focus was selecting and recommending applicants for the Youth Advisory Committee for the 2026-27 fiscal year.
keyTopics: Youth Advisory Committee member selection for 2026-27
Public comment period for residents
Approval of previous meeting minutes
Board and commission agenda planning

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 19, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a budget workshop to review Sunnyvale's spending plan for fiscal year 2026/27. Residents had the opportunity to speak to the council about the proposed budget before final approval.
keyTopics: FY 2026/27 budget proposal now available online for public review
Public comment period on city spending priorities
Budget workshop discussion and council feedback

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 19, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to update the city's Health and Safety plans for the next 15 years, recognized the Public Works Department for their work on roads, trees, facilities and other services, and celebrated Asian American Pacific Islander Month.
keyTopics: Update to Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Public Works Department recognition during Public Works Week
Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) Month celebration
City services including streets, sidewalks, trees, and facilities

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a special meeting of the Station Area Task Force on May 21, 2026, at City Hall to discuss issues related to station area development and planning. The meeting was open to the public both in person and online via Zoom.
keyTopics: Station Area Task Force special meeting convened
Meeting held at City Hall, 1500 Warburton Avenue
Public participation available in-person and via Zoom

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 19, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026 to approve several land development and zoning changes, confirm a special assessment for city services, and appoint a new permanent City Manager. The council conducted multiple public hearings to gather resident input on these decisions.
keyTopics: New zoning ordinance and urgency ordinance amendments
Site development permit and tentative map approval
Special assessment confirmation for city services
Appointment of permanent City Manager

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council discussed plans to help Palo Alto residents switch from gas to electric heating and appliances to comply with new air quality regulations. The council reviewed proposed principles for electrification programs and heard updates on the current status of residential electrification efforts.
keyTopics: Residential electrification programs to help residents upgrade to electric heating and appliances
Air District regulations requiring reduced gas use in homes
Proposed principles guiding how the city will support electrification
Current status and progress of electrification efforts in Palo Alto
